reason: The Usual Suspects revolves around morally ambiguous characters and a twisting narrative.
Pulp Fiction interweaves crime and dark humor with deeply flawed antiheroes.
Seven pits two detectives against a chilling killer in a gritty, morally gray world.
GoodFellas chronicles real-life mobsters with raw honesty and complex moral shades.
Natural Born Killers follows a murderous couple whose crimes blur right and wrong.
Taxi Driver portrays a lonely vigilante whose moral compass is unmistakably skewed.
Reservoir Dogs depicts a heist gone wrong where no one’s exactly the good guy.
Chinatown delves into a dark crime world where trust and virtue collapse.
Heat chronicles the cat-and-mouse game between a master thief and a relentless detective.
Donnie Brasco explores an undercover operation where loyalties and ethics collide.
